source_paper_title: Dynamic Portfolio Selection for Nonlinear Law-Dependent Preferences
context:
Page 15, Section 6 (Weighted utility), Remark 6.2(a); also stated in Abstract/Introduction (page 1 and page 3)

"It is acknowledged that there is no general well-posedness result for multi-dimensional QBSDEs." (p.3)

"It is worth noting that (6.4) is a two-dimensional QBSDE, whose two components \(\bar Y_1\) and \(\bar Y_2\) are coupled. Two-dimensional QBSDEs are technically much more challenging than the one-dimensional ones, and unless other assumptions are imposed, the well-posedness remains open; see the bibliographical notes in Subsection 7.4 of Zhang (2017)." (Remark 6.2(a), p.15)
title: Well-posedness of coupled multi-dimensional quadratic BSDEs arising from weighted-utility equilibrium
latex:
Consider a filtered probability space \((\Omega,\mathcal F,\mathbb F,\mathbb P)\) supporting a one-dimensional Brownian motion \(W\) on \([0,T]\). Let \(\kappa:[0,T]\times\Omega\to\mathbb R\) be a bounded \(\mathbb F\)-predictable process and define \(\Theta:=\int_0^T |\kappa(s)|^2\,ds\). Fix constants \(\gamma,\rho\) satisfying \(-1<\gamma\le 0\) and \(\gamma\le \rho<\gamma+1\). Define \(r_1:=\gamma\), \(r_2:=1-\rho+\gamma\), \(\lambda_1:=-\frac{\gamma}{1-\rho}\), \(\lambda_2:=\frac{1-\rho+\gamma}{1-\rho}\). Let \(C_i\in\mathbb R^{2\times 2}\), \(c_{j,i}\in\mathbb R\), and \(b_i\in\mathbb R\) (for \(i,j\in\{1,2\}\)) be the constants appearing in the two-dimensional quadratic BSDE system
\[
\begin{aligned}
 d\bar Y_i(s) &= -\tfrac12\Big( \bar Z(s)^\dagger C_i\bar Z(s) + c_{i,i}\,\bar Z_i(s)\kappa(s) + c_{-i,i}\,\bar Z_{-i}(s)\kappa(s) + b_i|\kappa(s)|^2\Big)ds \\
 &\quad + \bar Z_i(s)\,dW_s, \qquad s\in[0,T],\ i=1,2,\\
 \bar Y_1(T)&=\bar Y_2(T)=0,
\end{aligned}
\]
where \(\bar Z(s)=(\bar Z_1(s),\bar Z_2(s))^\dagger\) and \(-i\) denotes the index in \(\{1,2\}\setminus\{i\}\). The problem is to establish general conditions (beyond special structural cases or smallness assumptions such as \(\sup_{\tau}\|\Theta-\mathbb E_\tau[\Theta]\|_\infty\) being small) guaranteeing existence and uniqueness of a solution \((\bar Y,\bar Z)\) with \(\bar Y\in (L^\infty(\mathcal F,\mathbb R))^2\) and \(\bar Z\in (\mathrm{H}_{\mathrm{BMO}})^2\) for this coupled two-dimensional quadratic BSDE.
location_in_paper: Page 15, Section 6 (Weighted utility), Remark 6.2(a); also stated in Abstract/Introduction (page 1 and page 3)
open_problem_quote:
"It is acknowledged that there is no general well-posedness result for multi-dimensional QBSDEs." (p.3)

"It is worth noting that (6.4) is a two-dimensional QBSDE, whose two components \(\bar Y_1\) and \(\bar Y_2\) are coupled. Two-dimensional QBSDEs are technically much more challenging than the one-dimensional ones, and unless other assumptions are imposed, the well-posedness remains open; see the bibliographical notes in Subsection 7.4 of Zhang (2017)." (Remark 6.2(a), p.15)
